title: Reading and Use of English - Part 8
description: Read the four texts about language learning. For questions 47 – 56, choose from the people (A – D).

texts.A:
### The Perfectionist (Sanjay)
Sanjay spent three years studying French grammar before he dared to speak. [51]'I was paralyzed by the fear of making a mistake,' he admits.[/51] [49]He found that traditional textbooks were often out of touch with how people actually speak on the streets of Paris.[/49] [54]His breakthrough came when he started watching French stand-up comedy, which helped him understand the rhythm and slang of the language.[/54] Now, he is more comfortable, but he still feels a sense of frustration when he can't express complex philosophical ideas as clearly as he does in English.
texts.B:
### The Immersionist (Elena)
Elena moved to Italy with zero knowledge of the language. [47]'I deliberately avoided English-speaking expats to force myself to survive,' she says.[/47] [53]The first few months were exhausting; I would come home with a literal headache from the mental effort of translating.[/53] [50]She believes that the best way to learn is through the 'necessity of the moment'—ordering coffee or fixing a leak.[/50] [55]She notes that her personality seems to change slightly when she speaks Italian[/55]; she becomes more expressive and animated than her usual reserved self.
texts.C:
### The App-User (Hiro)
Hiro uses a popular language app to learn Spanish. [52]The 'gamification' of the process—earning points and maintaining streaks—is what keeps me coming back every morning.[/52] [48]However, he admits that while his vocabulary is vast, he struggles to hold a real-life conversation because the app doesn't prepare him for the speed of native speakers.[/48] [56]He also worries that he is becoming too dependent on the multiple-choice format[/56], which doesn't reflect the creative challenge of constructing sentences from scratch in the real world.
texts.D:
### The Polyglot (Clara)
Clara speaks six languages and views each as a 'different window' into the world. [49]She points out that certain concepts are simply untranslatable[/49], and you can only understand them if you speak the language. [51]She dismisses the idea that some people have a 'gift' for languages[/51]; she insists it’s just about time and consistency. [54]For her, the most effective technique is 'shadowing'—repeating audio recordings of native speakers immediately after hearing them[/54] to perfect the accent. She warns that if you don't use a language daily, it begins to 'rust' almost immediately.

questions.question: mention isolating themselves from their own language group to improve?
admit that a specific method failed to prepare them for the pace of real-life speech?
questions.explanation: Hiro says the app doesn't prepare him for the speed of native speakers.

questions.question: mention that some ideas can only be fully grasped in their original language?
questions.explanation: Clara points out that certain concepts are 'untranslatable'.

questions.question: believe that practical, everyday needs are the strongest motivation for learning?
questions.explanation: Elena believes the best way is through the 'necessity of the moment'.

questions.question: disagree with the notion that language learning is an innate talent?
questions.explanation: Clara dismisses the idea that people have a 'gift' (Sanjay A also mentions fear, but Clara D explicitly denies the 'gift').

questions.question: point out the addictive quality of a modern learning tool?
questions.explanation: Hiro mentions 'gamification' and 'streaks' keeping him coming back.

questions.question: describe a physical sensation of fatigue caused by the learning process?
questions.explanation: Elena mentions coming home with a 'literal headache'.

questions.question: mention using entertainment or imitation as a key learning strategy?
questions.explanation: Sanjay (A) uses stand-up comedy; Clara (D) uses 'shadowing' (imitation).

questions.question: observe a shift in their own character when using a different language?
questions.explanation: Elena notes her personality changes and she becomes more expressive.

questions.question: worry that they are relying too much on a structured, artificial format?
questions.explanation: Hiro worries about being too dependent on the 'multiple-choice format'.
